short_summary: Oil prices ease on Iran deal
rationale: Falling oil prices from the US-Iran ceasefire announcement offer mild indirect benefit to Portugal's energy-import-dependent economy, but the deal is not yet signed — continuation of last cycle's baseline.

top_events.title: US-Iran announce ceasefire/peace deal; oil prices fall
top_events.summary: Lower oil prices could reduce Portugal's energy import costs and ease cost-of-living pressures, but the deal remains unconfirmed — continuation of last cycle with no new qualitative change.

top_events.title: UK and Japan agree £18bn investment deal
top_events.summary: A major UK-Japan investment deal has limited direct relevance to Portugal, though it signals continued global trade activity that may indirectly affect EU economic sentiment.

top_events.title: Tourist train overturns and injures 17 during Cártama tapas festival
top_events.summary: A tourist train accident in Cártama, Spain (near Málaga) is geographically close to Portugal and relevant to the Iberian tourism sector, but impact on Portuguese daily life is minimal.
